action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 scored 0.7861, well below the best 0.8514 from iteration 9. The best recipe is clearly iteration 9's ConvNeXt-Tiny with natural CE + drop_path=0.1 + layer-wise LR for 20 epochs, followed by post-hoc logit adjustment. Analysis: Rather than chasing a radically different training recipe, the most reliable way to beat 0.8514 is to create a diverse ensemble. I will keep the proven iteration 9 checkpoint (best_iter9.pt) and train a second ConvNeXt-Tiny model with a slightly different regularization stack (Mixup α=0.2, label smoothing=0.1, drop_path=0.15, TrivialAugmentWide, seed=123) to encourage diversity. After training, I'll grid-search the ensemble weight and logit-adjustment temperature on the validation set, save a joint ensemble checkpoint, and update predict.py to load it. Decision: Write new train.py with Mixup/label smoothing, new ensemble_tune.py, and ensemble-capable predict.py. Run training, ensemble tuning, smoke test, and submit eval in one iteration.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 20 --drop_path 0.15 --mixup 0.2 --label_smoothing 0.1 --output_name mixup.pt --seed 123 && python ensemble_tune.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/mixup.pt --output checkpoints/ens_adj.pt && mkdir -p /tmp/fake_test && cp /data/val.pt /tmp/fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ens_adj.pt --output /tmp/fake_test/out.json && echo "Smoke test OK"
